currentSupportedBoundary.supportedFixture: Todo Search whitespace-normalization bug_fix dry-run fixture
currentSupportedBoundary.humanDecisionScope: current Todo Search fixture, current generated candidate, and current promotion review packet only

changeType.reason: The draft is recognized as behavior-change shaped for calibration policy only while preserving current bug_fix support boundaries.

evidenceShape.reason: The draft uses external project test, dogfood report, graph delta, and graph update proposal evidence rather than the Todo Search runtime/read-model evidence chain.

riskShape.reason: The draft needs escaping/stringification and observed-intent-not-maintainer-approval risk detail beyond the current generic risk vocabulary.

scopeShape.reason: The draft scope includes external checkout paths under work/external/escape-html, which are not committed repository paths.

contextShape.reason: The graph snapshot can point to graph source, change record, instruction pack, graph delta, proposal, and report artifacts, but source/test/README anchor mapping is not yet a supported compiler input surface.

validationCheckMapping.reason: External checks such as check-escape-html-npm-test and check-escape-html-dogfood-validator are previewed against observed dogfood Evidence, but they remain outside the current compiler required-check registry and are blocked by unresolved external checkout path authority.

outputRequirementSourceShape.reason: The existing output requirement source model can describe changed-file, command-output, validation-summary, and boundary obligations, but graph delta changed-file bindings are review artifacts rather than actual git diff authority.

unsupportedReasons.reason: The supported compiler command still reads only the Todo Search dry-run input path.
The current human decision is scoped to the Todo Search fixture and does not approve this calibration fixture.
The source dogfood is behavior-change shaped and recognized as a calibration draft, but this does not add supported behavior-change compilation.
The draft check IDs for npm test, dogfood validation, and graph-delta review now have preview bindings, but those previews are not current compiler required-check IDs or CI gates.
Allowed scope points at a local external checkout under work/external/escape-html rather than committed repository paths.

blockedReasonsIfWired.reason: External check binding is previewed, but current required-check generation cannot treat it as authoritative until external checkout path authority and a supported check registry extension exist.
Current source-authority validation expects committed fixture paths for compiler input cross references.
The draft preserves detailed calibration risk types in auxiliary fields because the current risk vocabulary is intentionally narrow.

sourceAuthorityReuse.reason: Changed files, command Evidence, validation summary, and boundary statements fit the existing source model, but graph delta changed files are review artifacts and not execution authority.
Policy-derived forbidden scope maps well to no metadata, no upstream approval, and no graph apply boundaries, but external checkout paths need explicit calibration handling.
Missing checkout, missing test command, and scope expansion map to existing stop source concepts, but the external check IDs are unsupported.
The fixture can map external_project_test_result, dogfood_report, and graph_delta_report to preview check bindings, but those bindings remain non-authoritative and outside compiler support.
Graph artifacts are available, but README/source/test anchor-level context is not yet a supported compiler context surface.
Generic source-authority-loss and scope-drift types can carry the draft, but stringification, escaping, and maintainer-approval risk detail needs a bounded vocabulary extension.
Allowed code/test paths are external local checkout paths, not committed repo fixture files.

sourceAuthorityGaps.recommendedFollowUp: Use the previewed binding as calibration evidence only; decide external checkout path authority before any supported required-check registry extension.
Represent external checkout paths as observed external targets without treating them as committed repo paths or execution authority.
Add bounded calibration risk vocabulary for escaping/stringification compatibility and maintainer-approval boundaries only if needed.
Model README/source/test anchors as context bindings without requiring direct source checkout mutation.
Keep graph delta and graph update proposal bindings review-only and distinct from graph apply authority.

recommendedNextStep.summary: Decide whether external checkout path authority should be the next v0.3 calibration scope.
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Behavior-change policy is recognized and external required-check binding is previewed, but observed checks remain blocked by non-authoritative work/external paths.

promotionEligibility.reason: The second fixture is calibration-only and has source-authority gaps. It has no promotion review packet or human decision.

nonExecutionBoundary: This observation is non-enforcing preview Evidence only. It does not mark the second fixture as supported, compile a candidate, execute AI, apply graph deltas, approve promotion, accept work, enable required checks, configure branch protection, or retire tree-native artifacts.
